highlights.text: Readers were prepared because for the 1st time in weeks my Bear Case triggered and we began a large sell.
I wrote on Monday at 4pm: “Bear case tomorrow: Begins under 6857… I t will probably be 6850 trigger.
Readers know that three of those buildings are what I call “elevator down” into “short squeeze” into “back-test”.
Elevator down refers to when ES loses a well-tested, previously defended support shelf and begins a sell.
Typically these sells are elevator down and cut through every support with ease.
Every elevator down sell in ES is followed by a violent squeeze.
Short squeezes are caused by my core setup: The Failed Breakdown or Level Reclaim.
This afternoon, ES swept Tuesday’s 6748 daily low, put in a Failed Breakdown, and started a bounce.

post_title: One Full Week Of Correction For SPX. How Much Do Bears Have Left? November 7 Plan
